title: How corruption risk changed across Mexican administrations
subtitle: Comparing procurement risk, direct award rates, and high-risk contracts across five presidential terms. Higher direct awards and risk scores indicate weaker competitive safeguards.

sexenio.heading: Administration comparison
sexenio.description: Each bar represents a full presidential term (sexenio). Click a metric below to switch the view.

directAward.label: AMLO direct awards
directAward.body: {{amloRate}}% of contracts under AMLO were awarded without competition, versus {{calderonRate}}% under Calderón. This removes the competitive safeguard that deters price manipulation.

riskIncrease.label: Risk score increase
riskIncrease.body: Average risk per contract rose from {{calderonRisk}}% under Calderón to {{amloRisk}}% under AMLO — the largest cross-administration jump in the dataset.

highRisk.label: AMLO high-risk share
highRisk.body: {{amloHighRisk}}% of AMLO-era contracts score as high or critical risk, compared to {{foxHighRisk}}% under Fox. RUBLI risk scores reflect similarity to documented corruption patterns.

timeline.heading: Year-by-year risk trend (2006–2025)
timeline.description: Red line: avg risk score. Orange area: % high-risk contracts. Blue dashed: direct award rate. Dashed vertical lines mark major procurement scandals.
timeline.footnote: Risk scores measure similarity to documented corruption patterns — not proof of wrongdoing. Direct award data before 2010 is incomplete (Structure A coverage gap).
